ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 represents bolognese sauce as a category; the full dish (pasta with beef bolognese) is matched at closest parent level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Classic Beef Bolognese is approximately 380 g, comprising a portion of cooked pasta topped with a generous beef and tomato meat sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Spaghetti with meat sauce (bolognese), homemade-style — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for spaghetti with meat sauce (bolognese-style), cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entries for cooked pasta and ground beef. The restaurant version at Harry's Boat Quay is a Western-style beef bolognese pasta; values represent a cooked mixed dish per 100 g edible portion. Sodium is elevated to reflect restaurant preparation with added salt and stock.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — spaghetti with meat sauce analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Classic Beef Bolognese. GI of 45 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for spaghetti with meat sauce (bolognese-style), which reports GI values in the range 40–52 (low category) due to the moderating effect of protein, fat, and tomato-based sauce on starch digestion. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 x 60.0) / 100 = 27.0, using 380 g serving with 15.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.guidance: A 380 g restaurant serving of Classic Beef Bolognese provides approximately 1216 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; patients should request reduced-salt preparation or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from pasta carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 45 (low) but a glycemic load of 27 per standard serving, Classic Beef Bolognese has a moderate overall glycemic impact; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and prefer whole-grain pasta variants to further reduce postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from beef and restaurant seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Classic Beef Bolognese contains approximately 7.2 g saturated fat and 1216 mg sodium per standard serving; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and consider lean-beef or plant-protein substitutions to reduce saturated fat intake in line with AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Classic Beef Bolognese is a traditional Italian-origin pasta dish consisting of long pasta (typically spaghetti or tagliatelle) served with a slow-simmered meat sauce made from ground beef, tomatoes, onion, garlic, carrot, celery, red wine, and beef stock. The sauce is cooked low and slow to develop a rich, savoury, umami-forward flavour with a thick, meaty texture that clings to the pasta. In Singapore, it is commonly served as a Western comfort-food dish in casual dining restaurants and bars such as Harry's. Nutritionally, a standard restaurant serving (~380 g) provides approximately 540 kcal, 31 g protein, 19 g fat, and 60 g carbohydrate. The dish is a moderate source of iron, zinc, and B vitamins from the beef component, and provides lycopene from the tomato sauce. Clinically, the primary concerns are moderate sodium content from restaurant preparation (relevant for hypertension), moderate glycemic load from pasta carbohydrates (relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management), and saturated fat from ground beef (relevant for cardiovascular disease risk). The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or dairy allergies. It is not certified halal due to the use of beef and wine in preparation.
common_user_queries: How many calories in beef bolognese?
Is beef bolognese high in sodium?
What pasta is used in bolognese?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for spaghetti with meat sauce (bolognese-style) as no exact Singapore-specific or HPB Foodo entry exists for Classic Beef Bolognese. The adaptation is defensible given the standard composition of the dish, but restaurant-specific variation in portion size, fat content, and sodium cannot be fully controlled without laboratory analysis.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA FoodData Central source for this dish type and is not a primary nutrient of concern for bolognese. All other -1 values have been replaced with estimated values from USDA FoodData Central composite data. Cuisine tag in input JSON was listed as 'Burger' which appears to be a GrabFood platform categorisation error; the dish is correctly classified as Western Italian-origin pasta. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for spaghetti with meat sauce; no direct measurement for this specific dish exists. Glycemic load per serving = (45 x 15.8 x 380) / (100 x 100) = 27.0 g. Halal status is marked false as the dish traditionally contains wine and the restaurant (Harry's Boat Quay) is a bar venue; halal certification is not confirmed. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as this dish is served in Singapore context.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is canonical English 'Classic Beef Bolognese'; local names provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singapore-served dish.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal 142.0, protein_g 8.2, fat_g 5.1, carbohydrate_g 15.8) are present, numeric, and sourced from USDA FoodData Central adapted composite for spaghetti with meat sauce.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.4), sodium_mg (320.0), saturated_fat_g (1.9), and sugar_g (3.2) are all present as numeric values supported by USDA FoodData Central source.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (45), gi_category ('low'), glycemic_load_per_serving (27.0), and gi_source are all present. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for spaghetti with meat sauce, documented in gi_source.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (bolognese sauce, closest_parent) and FoodEx2 A0CKW (pasta with meat sauce, closest_parent) provide defensible ontology anchors. SNOMED CT is null with documented gap note. No codes are invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters and covers food identity, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context in Singapore, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate, sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate, glycemic load), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate, saturated fat and sodium). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.
